method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting — brooklynda.org/robots.txt is a Yoast block with no disallows). Fingerprinted www.brooklynda.org from response headers and page markup: Apache + WordPress 6.8.5 + GeneratePress/GP Premium theme, with Wordfence, Yoast, MonsterInsights (Google Analytics), MetaSlider, FileBird, Redirection, UserFeedback, Google Language Translator, and Embed Any Document. Enumerated the WordPress REST API (/wp-json) namespaces and the wp/v2 content categories (Press Releases = 911 posts). Verified via the Socrata Discovery API that NO NYC Open Data asset exists under any Brooklyn/Kings County DA agency label (resultSetSize 0 for every variant).
headline_findings: The Brooklyn DA is a content-only domain with no data: a single self-hosted WordPress 6.8.5 brochure site (brooklynda.org, Apache, GeneratePress, Wordfence) — no portal, no application, no data platform.
The only machine-readable surface is the WordPress REST API (/wp-json/wp/v2), an accidental content API that exposes ~911 press releases plus notifications, statements, and bureau/program pages.

There is ZERO NYC Open Data for any DA office — the Socrata Discovery API returns no assets for every Brooklyn/Kings County DA label. The core justice data (caseloads, dispositions, diversions, conviction-review outcomes) is published nowhere as data, only as prose inside press releases.
No inbound machine surface: submitting a tip — the everyday inbound transaction — has only a captcha-gated web form, a phone hotline, or a bureau email.

All five NYC county DA offices share the same functions (bureaus, diversion, conviction review, victim services, press). This is a candidate for ONE shared DA API, built once and adopted five times. Net-new write surface: submit a confidential tip (TipSubmission). See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

apis_observed.note: The one real machine-readable surface. ~911 press releases (?categories=pr) plus notifications, statements, and bureau/program pages. Accidental — CMS default, raw WordPress fields, not a designed resource model.
ZERO datasets under any Brooklyn/Kings County DA label (verified via Socrata Discovery API). The office publishes no open data.
Only inbound path for a tip/complaint besides phone/email. reCAPTCHA/hCaptcha/Turnstile per Permissions-Policy header. No JSON, no documented submit endpoint.
Bureaus, programs, press releases, brochures, resources. NOT on the nyc.gov shared platform — the office runs its own domain.

fruit.name: Press Releases, Statements & Notifications
fruit.notes: The office's only substantial published data. Reachable via the CMS default content API — an accident, not a design. Raw WordPress fields, no resource model.

fruit.name: Programs, Bureaus & Initiatives
fruit.notes: Bureaus, diversion and re-entry programs, Conviction Review Unit, Red Hook Community Court, Young Adult Court, Virtual Currency Unit, etc. Exist only as unstructured WordPress pages; no structured program directory.

fruit.name: Community Resources (brochures, palm cards, hotlines)
fruit.notes: Elder abuse, human trafficking, tenant protection, deed fraud, anti-bullying, immigrant affairs — many in 'all languages' variants (Google Language Translator + PDF embeds). No structured directory.

fruit.name: Victim Services
fruit.notes: Advocacy, safety planning, court accompaniment, case notification, compensation help, language access — described within bureau pages (Domestic Violence, Hate Crimes, Human Trafficking, Elder Abuse, Immigrant Affairs). Unstructured prose.

fruit.name: Case / Prosecution Statistics (aggregate)
fruit.notes: The office's biggest gap. Caseloads, arraignments, dispositions, declined prosecutions, diversions, and conviction-review outcomes are published NOWHERE as data — only as prose inside press releases. Aggregate-only by design; individual cases must never be exposed. Proposed net-new data surface.

fruit.name: Submit a confidential tip
fruit.notes: The everyday inbound transaction — report a fraud, an unsafe landlord, a hate incident, trafficking, or information on an open matter. No API and no Open Data twin; lives only behind a reCAPTCHA/hCaptcha web form, a phone hotline, or a bureau email. The net-new WRITE surface for this domain.

meta.platform: WordPress 6.8.5 (GeneratePress / GP Premium) on Apache; Wordfence, Yoast, MonsterInsights; accidental WordPress REST API (wp/v2)
meta.netnew: TipSubmission — submit and track a confidential tip (report fraud, an unsafe landlord, a hate incident, trafficking, or information on an open case)
meta.tagline: A WordPress brochure site with no data, an accidental content API, and no way to send in a tip — one of five identical county DA offices.
meta.headline: The Brooklyn DA publishes no open data at all; its only machine-readable surface is the accidental WordPress REST API over ~911 press releases, so the modernization move is to template one shared, agent-native API across all five NYC county DA offices.

scorecard_notes.platform: Current WordPress 6.8.5 but brochureware on a heavy third-party plugin stack; no application tier.
scorecard_notes.siteApi: Only the accidental WordPress REST API (wp/v2) — real but undesigned, content-only, raw CMS fields.
scorecard_notes.openData: Zero NYC Open Data assets under any Brooklyn/Kings County DA label (verified).
scorecard_notes.coreData: Caseloads, dispositions, diversions, conviction-review outcomes published nowhere as data — only prose in press releases.
scorecard_notes.writeApi: No inbound API; tips go through a captcha web form, phone hotline, or bureau email.
scorecard_notes.agent: No agent-native surface of any kind; no MCP, no structured contract.
scorecard_notes.ownership: Owns its domain and content, but every data/security/analytics capability is a rented WordPress plugin; no owned API.
